description:
Replace the typer's single-FnTypes-per-FnIr model with per-callsite specialization keyed by `(FnId, input-Descr-tuple)`. Each unique input signature gets its own typed body, its own Cranelift FuncId, its own Schema. The lub-aggregation that widens self-recursive returns to `any` disappears; monomorphic flows (the common case) get monomorphic ABIs end-to-end.

## Motivation

fz's typer currently joins all callsites' arg Descrs into one Descr per param, then computes one return Descr. For a self-recursive fn like `fact`, the fixed point widens to `any` because the lub doesn't narrow. This forces `.27.x`-style workarounds:

- ir_typer.rs:148-151 — explicit "leave cont slot 0 at `any`" hack
- ir_typer.rs:166-168 — closure-reachability skip
- The natively_callable cascade exists in part to handle conts with `any` captures
- .27.5.4.1's planned final_return_kinds infrastructure is a workaround for the same root cause

All of these are symptoms of the wrong typer key. Per-callsite specialization eliminates them.

## What specialization does

For each Call site, the typer instantiates the callee's body with the actual arg Descrs at that site. Different callsites produce different (FnTypes, return Descr, Schema, Signature, compiled body). Recursive calls converge via fixed point over (fn, key) pairs (same shape as today's fixed point, finer key).

Result: `fact(int)` returns `int`. fact's body is monomorphically int → goes native end-to-end. Cont frames disappear from typed flows. The dispatch fallbacks that exist for `any` flows still exist for genuinely dynamic paths (closures, message passing) but fire vastly less often.

## Why this is the right shape

- No missing analysis — every input is already available (callsite arg Descrs, the body IR, type_fn with input-constraint support). The change is re-keying, not new inference.
- Closures, Spawn, Receive remain dynamic-dispatch points; they fall back to the `any`-key specialization. Code size growth is bounded.
- Runtime-facing types unchanged: the runtime keys on `schema_id` (opaque u32), not FnId. Multiple specializations per FnId means multiple schema_ids — the runtime doesn't notice.

## Code paths this touches (from pre-flight investigation)

Eight codegen-internal structures currently keyed by `FnId.0` become per-specialization:
- ModuleTypes, schemas, fn_sigs, fn_ids, fn_ptrs, frame_sizes, return_descrs, idx_of

Plan: introduce an internal `SpecId(u32)` during the typer pass; keep these as Vecs indexed by SpecId; one `HashMap<(FnId, SpecKey), SpecId>` lookup at callsite resolution. SpecId maps directly to the runtime's existing schema_id.

## What disappears

- `ir_typer.rs:148-151` — leave-at-any hack
- `ir_typer.rs:166-168` — closure-reachability skip becomes "use the any-key"
- `.27.5.4.1`'s planned final_return_kinds fixed-point — never built
- Various redundant unions in the narrowed-aggregation pass

## What stays

- `natively_callable` analysis (operates over the expanded set; logic unchanged)
- The heap-safe-captures cascade (correctness machinery; inputs are tighter)
- The descr_is_int/float/atom quartet and its ~30 callsites (they read per-specialization FnTypes already)
- `.27.12` stack maps (orthogonal)

## Atoms

- .29.1 — Re-key ModuleTypes; fixed point over (FnId, key); no codegen behavior change yet.
- .29.2 — SpecId indirection + 8 keyed-structure conversion + per-spec compile_fn.
- .29.3 — Closures/Spawn/Receive fall back to the any-key specialization.
- .29.4 — Kill the workarounds; verify the heap-safe cascade still terminates; update 13 typer unit tests.
- .29.5 — (standalone, post-arc) closure-callsite flow analysis; subsumes .27.5.6 part A.
- .29.6 — (standalone, post-arc) runtime closure-invoke kind-aware copy; from .27.5.6 part B.

## Supersedes

- fz-ul4.27.5.4.1 — closed as superseded; the multi-callsite agreement problem doesn't exist under specialization.
- fz-ul4.27.5.6 — split between .29.5 (part A) and .29.6 (part B).
title: [EPIC] Per-callsite typer specialization
